questions.text: Which command shows current kernel version?

options.text: Follow change control and monitor metrics

questions.explanation: uname -r prints the running kernel release.

questions.text: Which file defines static hostname on systemd systems?
questions.explanation: /etc/hostname sets the system hostname.

questions.text: Which command installs packages on RHEL-family systems?
questions.explanation: RHEL derivatives use dnf/yum.

questions.text: Which permission octal is rwx for owner, r-- group/other?

options.text: Follow change control and monitor metrics

questions.explanation: 7= rwx, 4= r-- gives 744.

questions.text: Which systemd command enables service at boot?
questions.explanation: enable creates boot-time symlink.

questions.text: Which partition should mount /home separately for user data?

options.text: Follow change control and monitor metrics

questions.explanation: Separate /home eases reinstalls and quotas.

questions.text: Which command lists block devices and filesystems?
questions.explanation: lsblk shows disks, partitions, and FS types.

questions.text: Which file configures default boot loader entries?
questions.explanation: GRUB defaults live in /etc/default/grub.

questions.text: Which RAID level stripes with parity?
questions.explanation: RAID 5 uses distributed parity.

questions.text: Which command adds user with home directory on Linux?

options.text: Follow change control and monitor metrics

questions.explanation: useradd -m creates home directory.

questions.explanation: VG pools PVs for LV allocation.

questions.explanation: User crontabs are edited with crontab -e.

questions.text: Which command shows mounted filesystems?

questions.explanation: SIGTERM allows cleanup before exit.

questions.explanation: fdisk/parted manage partition tables.

questions.explanation: apt, dnf, and zypper manage distro packages.

questions.text: Which file stores hashed passwords?
questions.explanation: Shadow stores password hashes readable by root.

questions.text: Which command sets SELinux mode enforcing temporarily?
questions.explanation: setenforce toggles enforcing/permissive until reboot if not persistent.

questions.text: Which SSH config disables root login?
questions.explanation: Disabling root SSH reduces brute force risk.

questions.text: Which firewall tool on RHEL 8+ is default?
questions.explanation: firewalld manages zones on RHEL family.

questions.text: Which permission on private key file is recommended?
questions.explanation: Private keys should be user-read/write only.

questions.text: Which PAM module can deny login after failures?
questions.explanation: Account lockout modules limit guessing.

questions.text: Which command lists SELinux context of file?
questions.explanation: ls -Z shows SELinux security context.

questions.text: Which GPG use case signs packages in repo?
questions.explanation: Signed repos prevent tampered packages.

questions.text: Which sudoers directive runs command as root?
questions.explanation: sudo grants elevated commands with audit.

questions.text: Which audit framework logs syscalls on Linux?
questions.explanation: auditd records security-relevant events.

questions.text: Which SSH key type is modern default?
questions.explanation: Ed25519 offers strong security and performance.

questions.text: Which file limits login attempts in sshd?
questions.explanation: MaxAuthTries caps authentication attempts.

questions.text: Which command updates CA trust store on RHEL?
questions.explanation: update-ca-trust refreshes system CAs.

questions.text: Which kernel parameter hardens IP forwarding when not router?
questions.explanation: Disable forwarding on non-router hosts.

questions.text: Which tool scans packages for known CVEs?
questions.explanation: Package scanners match versions to CVE databases.

questions.text: Which access control uses MAC labels on RHEL?
questions.explanation: SELinux enforces mandatory access control.

questions.text: Which fail2ban action modifies firewall?
questions.explanation: fail2ban triggers firewall blocks on abuse.

questions.text: Which file should never be world-writable?
questions.explanation: Critical system files must not be world-writable.

questions.text: Which TWO harden SSH? (Select TWO.)

options.text: Change default port optionally plus controls

questions.explanation: Keys, strong ciphers, and hardening reduce SSH risk.

questions.explanation: Playbooks declare tasks and roles.
